3.happening: Measure the image — how many rows it has.
3.why: We need the bounds to test neighbors.
3.invariant: The image's dimensions are fixed for the whole run.

4.happening: Measure how many columns each row has.
4.why: Column count bounds our left/right moves.
4.invariant: Every row has the same width.

5.happening: Remember the start pixel's original color ({start_color}).
5.why: We only recolor pixels that share this exact color.
5.invariant: start_color is the color being replaced.

6.why: We must remember which pixels we've recolored so none is refilled.

7.happening: Loop over each row to give it a fresh tracker row.
7.why: state must mirror the image's shape.
7.invariant: Each built row holds exactly {cols} zeros.

8.happening: Add a row of zeros — every pixel starts unseen (0).
8.invariant: No pixel is marked before the fill begins.

9.happening: Reset the current-row marker to -1 (no active pixel).
9.why: There's an active pixel only while filling.

10.why: Same — nothing is being filled yet.
10.invariant: cur_r and cur_c together locate the active pixel.

11.happening: List the four orthogonal moves (up, down, right, left).
11.why: Fill spreads only horizontally or vertically.
11.invariant: Only these four directions are ever tried.

12.happening: The start pixel is already color {color} — no fill needed.
12.why: Recoloring it to the same color would loop forever.
12.invariant: An identical color means the image is already done.

12.happening: The start pixel is color {start_color}, not {color} — we'll flood the region.
12.why: The colors differ, so there's real work to do.
12.invariant: Only pixels of color {start_color} will change.

13.why: The start pixel already had the target color.

14.happening: Make an empty stack for the pixels to recolor.
14.why: The stack holds discovered pixels not yet explored.
14.invariant: Depth-first: we always explore the most recent pixel next.

15.happening: Push the start pixel ({sr},{sc}) onto the stack.
15.why: The fill spreads outward from here.
15.invariant: The stack holds only the fill frontier.

16.happening: Mark the start pixel as seen (1).
16.why: So it can never be pushed again.
16.invariant: A pixel enters the stack at most once.

17.why: It's the first pixel of the filled region.
17.invariant: Recolored pixels now hold the new color.

18.happening: The stack holds {len(stack)} pixel(s) still to explore — keep filling.
18.why: Unexplored frontier remains in the region.
18.invariant: The stack empties as the region fills.

18.happening: The stack is empty — the whole region is recolored.
18.why: No connected pixel of the original color remains.

19.happening: Pop the top pixel off the stack.
19.why: Depth-first explores the most recently found pixel first.
19.invariant: The popped pixel is about to become the active pixel.

20.why: We need its coordinates to reach neighbors.

21.why: Row and column together locate the active pixel.
21.invariant: cur_r, cur_c now point at the pixel being filled.

22.happening: Mark the active pixel ({cur_r},{cur_c}) fully done (2).
22.why: It's been recolored and its neighbors are next.

23.happening: Try each of the four directions from this pixel.
23.why: To find adjacent pixels of the original color.
23.invariant: All four neighbors are examined before moving on.

24.why: The direction's row offset added to the active pixel.
24.invariant: nr may be off-grid — we check next.

25.why: The direction's column offset added to the active pixel.
25.invariant: nc may be off-grid — we check next.

26.when: nr >= 0 && nr < rows && nc >= 0 && nc < cols && image[nr][nc] == start_color
26.happening: Neighbor ({nr},{nc}) still has the original color {start_color} — recolor it.
26.why: It's in-bounds and matches the color we're replacing.
26.invariant: Only original-color pixels join the region.

26.happening: Neighbor ({nr},{nc}) is off-grid or a different color — leave it.
26.why: Only pixels of the original color are part of the region.
26.invariant: Other colors and out-of-range cells are never changed.

27.happening: Mark the matching neighbor ({nr},{nc}) as seen (1).
27.why: Claim it before recoloring so it can't be pushed twice.
27.invariant: Seen pixels are waiting in the stack.

28.why: It shares the region's original color.

29.happening: Push the neighbor onto the stack.
29.why: So we'll spread the fill from it next.

30.happening: The region is fully recolored — return the image.
30.invariant: The returned image is the flood-filled result.

init.happening: Setting up: reading the start color and preparing an empty visited-tracker.
init.why: We need the original color and a way to remember filled pixels.

check.happening: Deciding whether the fill applies, or whether a neighbor matches.
check.why: We only recolor in-bounds pixels of the original color.
check.invariant: Each pixel is filled at most once.

update.happening: Recoloring a pixel and marking it part of the region.
update.why: State and image must reflect the growing fill.
update.invariant: A recolored pixel keeps the new color.

loop.happening: Exploring the frontier of the fill region.
loop.why: Depth-first walks the connected same-color pixels.

move.happening: Advancing the fill — popping a pixel and reaching toward neighbors.
move.why: The region spreads to adjacent matching pixels.

return.happening: Fill complete — returning the recolored image.
return.why: No connected original-color pixel remains (or none needed changing).
return.invariant: The image is the final answer.
